title: Team of Rivals
author: Doris Kearns Goodwin
subline: The political genius of Abraham Lincoln.
customHTML:
<svg viewBox="0 0 1600 2400" preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid slice" style="position:absolute;inset:0;width:100%;height:100%">
  <!-- Five portrait slots in a row, each tagged with a rival's surname. One is RED (Lincoln's principal rival, Seward). -->
  <g transform="translate(160 320)">
    <!-- Slot template: a hairline-framed box with a name beneath. Five slots, 5*260 = 1300px wide, with 16px gap = (1600-320-1300)/4 = 0px — so slots are 256px wide. -->
    <!-- We'll lay them out at x = 0, 256, 512, 768, 1024, each 240 wide, with 16 gap. Total 5*240 + 4*16 = 1264. Centered. -->
    <g font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" fill="#cccccc">
      <!-- Seward (RED - Lincoln's chief rival) -->
      <g transform="translate(0 0)">
        <rect x="0" y="0" width="240" height="900" fill="none" stroke="#fc1c46" stroke-width="2"/>
        <text x="120" y="980" text-anchor="middle" font-size="20" font-weight="500" fill="#fc1c46" letter-spacing="0.04em">SEWARD</text>
        <text x="120" y="1010" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="400" fill="#fc1c46" opacity="0.7" letter-spacing="0.04em">RIVAL · 1860</text>
      </g>
      <!-- Chase -->
      <g transform="translate(256 0)">
        <rect x="0" y="0" width="240" height="900" fill="none" stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="1"/>
        <text x="120" y="980" text-anchor="middle" font-size="20" font-weight="500" fill="#cccccc" letter-spacing="0.04em">CHASE</text>
        <text x="120" y="1010" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="400" fill="#4c4c4c" letter-spacing="0.04em">RIVAL · 1860</text>
      </g>
      <!-- Cameron -->
      <g transform="translate(512 0)">
        <rect x="0" y="0" width="240" height="900" fill="none" stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="1"/>
        <text x="120" y="980" text-anchor="middle" font-size="20" font-weight="500" fill="#cccccc" letter-spacing="0.04em">CAMERON</text>
        <text x="120" y="1010" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="400" fill="#4c4c4c" letter-spacing="0.04em">RIVAL · 1860</text>
      </g>
      <!-- Bates -->
      <g transform="translate(768 0)">
        <rect x="0" y="0" width="240" height="900" fill="none" stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="1"/>
        <text x="120" y="980" text-anchor="middle" font-size="20" font-weight="500" fill="#cccccc" letter-spacing="0.04em">BATES</text>
        <text x="120" y="1010" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="400" fill="#4c4c4c" letter-spacing="0.04em">RIVAL · 1860</text>
      </g>
      <!-- Stanton -->
      <g transform="translate(1024 0)">
        <rect x="0" y="0" width="240" height="900" fill="none" stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="1"/>
        <text x="120" y="980" text-anchor="middle" font-size="20" font-weight="500" fill="#cccccc" letter-spacing="0.04em">STANTON</text>
        <text x="120" y="1010" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="400" fill="#4c4c4c" letter-spacing="0.04em">RIVAL · 1860</text>
      </g>
    </g>
    <!-- Below the row: a thin connecting rule (the cabinet) and a single label 'CABINET' centered -->
    <line x1="0" y1="1060" x2="1264" y2="1060" stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="1"/>
    <text x="632" y="1100" text-anchor="middle" font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" font-size="16" font-weight="500" fill="#4c4c4c" letter-spacing="0.18em">LINCOLN'S CABINET · 1861</text>
  </g>
</svg>
rationale: The five rivals ARE the cover. Five vertical hairline-framed portrait slots, labeled with surnames (Seward, Chase, Cameron, Bates, Stanton), take the top 2/3 of the canvas. One — Seward, Lincoln's chief rival — is rendered in red 2px outline + red type; the other four are graphite 1px + ash. The row reads as a row of daguerreotypes / cabinet cards. The 280px mixed-case title 'Team of Rivals' sits below in weight 300. Pill '1860' anchors the top-right. The single red is Seward's frame — the book's central tension made literal.
